title: Ellipsis and substitution
explanation:
Ellipsis and substitution

We avoid repetition with auxiliary verbs, so/neither, and pronouns like one/ones, do so, such.

  • I enjoy hiking, and so does my sister.
  • He didn't call, nor did she.
  • I'd like the blue one, not the red one.

questions.text: She speaks French fluently, and so [*does|is|has] her brother.
I haven't finished the report, and neither [*have|has|do] my colleagues.
This laptop is faster than the old [*one|ones|it].
questions.feedback: One substitutes a singular countable noun.

questions.text: If you need help, please [*do so|do it so|do] immediately.
They promised to attend, but they didn't [*do|did|done].
questions.feedback: Auxiliary verb replaces the main verb phrase.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: He loves jazz, and so ____ his wife. | *does | is | has
I won't accept the offer, and neither ____ she. | *will | do | did
These shoes are more comfortable than the previous ____.  | *ones | one | pair one
She said she would call, but she never ____.  | *did | was | had
He didn't enjoy the film, and nor [*did|do|was] I.
I'll take the green scarf rather than the grey [*one|ones|it].
They asked us to leave early, and we [*did so|did it|were so].
She can swim well, but her brother [*can't|doesn't|isn't].
